name: Figma Design Tool Automation
description: Battle-tested playbook for Figma browser automation via CDP. Covers file browser navigation, editor tools (shapes, text, frames), layer management, properties panel, share dialog, design/prototype tabs, and export. Figma uses WebGL canvas — DOM mouse events don't work on canvas, but CDP Input.dispatchMouseEvent (browser_human_click) does.

share_dialog.email_input: input[aria-label='Add comma separated emails to invite']

keyboard_shortcuts.notes: Keyboard shortcuts only work when Figma canvas has focus. JS keydown events do NOT trigger Figma shortcuts — they must go through CDP Input.dispatchKeyEvent or native OS key events.

open_file_from_browser.description: Open a design file from the file browser home page
steps: Find thumbnail: [data-testid='thumbnail-container']
Get parent container with file name text to identify correct file
Double-click via JS: full mousedown+mouseup+click sequence with detail:1 then detail:2 + dblclick event
Wait for URL to change to /design/ or /board/
Wait for canvas element to appear
guards: Must be on file browser page
open_file_from_browser.why: Single click selects file (shows context menu). Double-click opens it. Figma's SPA uses custom event handlers — need full dblclick event sequence with proper detail counts. browser_human_click does single click only.

steps: Click new file button: [data-testid='new-design-file-button'] (or whiteboard/slides/etc)
Use browser_human_click on the button
Wait for URL to change to /design/ and canvas to load
guards: Must be on file browser page

select_tool.description: Activate a toolbar tool (Move, Frame, Rectangle, Pen, Text, Comment)
steps: Click tool button: [data-testid='{ToolName}-tool'] via browser_human_click
Verify: aria-pressed='true' on the clicked tool button
select_tool.why: browser_human_click reliably activates toolbar tools. The tool stays active until used or another tool is selected.

create_shape.description: Create a rectangle, frame, or other shape on canvas
steps: Activate tool: browser_human_click on [data-testid='Rectangle-tool'] (or Frame-tool, etc)
Verify tool is active: aria-pressed='true'
Click on canvas: browser_human_click on 'canvas' selector
This creates a default 100x100 element at the click position
Tool auto-deactivates (switches back to Move)
New layer appears in layers panel: [data-testid$='-layers-panel-row']
create_shape.why: CDP Input.dispatchMouseEvent (via browser_human_click) works on Figma's WebGL canvas. DOM-level mouse events (dispatchEvent MouseEvent) do NOT work. Single click creates default-sized shape; drag would create custom size but requires native drag or CDP mouse event sequences.

select_layer.description: Select a layer from the layers panel
steps: Find layer: [data-testid$='-layers-panel-row'] with matching text content
Use browser_human_click on the layer row
Verify: properties panel shows X-position, Y-position inputs with values
Selected layer's properties become editable in the right panel

modify_properties.description: Change element position, size, color, or other properties
steps: Select the target layer first (see select_layer flow)
Find the input: e.g. input[aria-label='X-position']
Use browser_fill_form with clear:true to type new value
Press Enter to commit: dispatch keydown Enter event on the input
Verify: re-read input value matches new value
modify_properties.why: browser_fill_form with CDP key events works reliably on Figma's property inputs. The value commits on Enter or blur.

steps: Click Share button: browser_human_click on [data-testid='multiplayer-toolbar-share-button']
Dialog appears with role='dialog' containing 'Share' text
Available actions: invite via email, copy link, copy prototype link, publish to community
open_share_dialog.why: Clicking Share button again closes the dialog (toggle behavior). Canvas click does NOT close it.

steps: Click Share button again: browser_human_click on [data-testid='multiplayer-toolbar-share-button']
Dialog closes
close_share_dialog.why: Share button toggles the dialog. Escape key via JS doesn't work. Canvas click doesn't close it either.

steps: Click: browser_human_click on button[aria-label='Main menu']
Menu items appear with role='menuitem'
Items: Back to files, Actions, File, Edit, View, Object, Text, Arrange, Vector, Plugins, Widgets, Preferences, Libraries, Help and account
open_main_menu.why: Menu auto-closes very quickly — you must read items immediately in the same tool call sequence. The menu is a toggle (click again to close).

switch_design_prototype_tab.description: Switch between Design and Prototype tabs in properties panel
steps: Find tab header: [data-testid='property-panel-tab-header']
Click Design or Prototype button within it
Verify: aria-selected='true' on the clicked tab

navigate_back_to_files.description: Go back to file browser from editor
steps: Click folder link: [data-testid='folder-name-link']
Wait for URL to change back to /files/
Click Present button: browser_human_click on [data-testid='present-as-prototype']
Prototype opens in new tab or overlay

canvas_interactions: browser_human_click cannot drag — use native drag or CDP mouse sequences for custom sizes
menu_navigation: browser_human_click to open, browser_js to read items quickly
file_opening: JS dblclick event sequence on thumbnail container
data_extraction: browser_js — query data-testid elements, aria-labels, input values
NOT_WORKING: JS dispatchEvent MouseEvent on canvas (WebGL ignores DOM events)
JS KeyboardEvent for keyboard shortcuts (Figma intercepts at lower level)
JS Escape to close dialogs (doesn't reach Figma's event handler)

errors.error: DOM mouse events don't work on Figma canvas
errors.context: Attempting to draw, click, or drag on the WebGL canvas using JS dispatchEvent(new MouseEvent(...))
errors.solution: Use CDP-level mouse events via browser_human_click. Figma's canvas is WebGL-based and only responds to real browser input events, not DOM-synthesized ones.

errors.error: JS keyboard shortcuts don't trigger Figma actions
errors.context: document.dispatchEvent(new KeyboardEvent('keydown', {key: 'r'})) does not activate Rectangle tool
errors.solution: Use browser_human_click on toolbar buttons instead. For shortcuts that must go through keyboard, use CDP Input.dispatchKeyEvent or native OS key events.

errors.error: File double-click doesn't work with browser_human_click
errors.context: browser_human_click dispatches a single click on thumbnail, which selects the file but doesn't open it
errors.solution: Use JS to dispatch full dblclick event sequence: mousedown(detail:1) + mouseup + click(detail:1) + mousedown(detail:2) + mouseup + click(detail:2) + dblclick(detail:2) on the thumbnail container or its parent card element.

errors.error: Main menu auto-closes before items can be read
errors.context: Opening main menu via browser_human_click, then reading items in a separate browser_js call — menu is already closed
errors.solution: Read menu items immediately in the same interaction. Or use direct button clicks for known actions (e.g. Share, Present) instead of navigating menus.

errors.error: Share dialog doesn't close with Escape or canvas click
errors.context: JS Escape keydown event doesn't reach Figma's dialog handler. Clicking canvas also doesn't dismiss the share dialog.
errors.solution: Click the Share button again to toggle the dialog closed.

errors.error: Property input shows 'Mixed' after value change
errors.context: After browser_fill_form + Enter on X-position input, value briefly shows 'Mixed'
errors.solution: This is a transient state when multiple elements are affected or during React re-render. Re-click the layer and re-read the input — the value should be correctly updated.

errors.context: cg.captureScreen timed out after 15000ms when Figma editor is open
errors.solution: Use browser_js to extract text content and element states instead of screenshots. For visual verification, use browser_dom or read data-testid attributes.

errors.context: mcp__sh__drag (cg.mouseDrag) times out when trying to draw shapes by dragging on canvas
errors.solution: Use single-click approach: activate shape tool + browser_human_click on canvas creates default 100x100 element. Resize via properties panel inputs afterward.

policyNotes.canvas_architecture: Figma uses WebGL (asm.js/Wasm) for rendering. The canvas element is a single <canvas> tag — there are no DOM elements for design objects. All interaction must go through CDP-level input events or toolbar/panel DOM elements.
policyNotes.auto_layout: Auto Layout options appear as radio buttons (NONE, VERTICAL, HORIZONTAL, GRID) in properties panel when a frame is selected.
policyNotes.layer_ids: Layer panel rows use data-testid format '{nodeId}-layers-panel-row' where nodeId matches Figma's internal node ID (e.g. '605:11').
policyNotes.tool_behavior: Shape tools (Rectangle, Frame) auto-deactivate after creating one element, returning to Move tool. Text tool stays active until explicitly switched.
policyNotes.mode_switching: Three modes available via toolbelt-mode-segmented-control: Draw (illustration), Design, Dev Mode (handoff).
safety: Add 1-2s delays between actions to allow Figma's WebSocket sync
Verify tool activation via aria-pressed before canvas interactions
Always select layers via layers panel rather than canvas clicks for reliability
Use browser_fill_form with clear:true for property inputs

key_discoveries: WebGL canvas only responds to CDP Input.dispatchMouseEvent, not DOM MouseEvent — browser_human_click works, JS dispatchEvent doesn't
Single CDP click with active shape tool creates default 100x100 element — no drag needed for basic creation
JS keyboard events don't trigger Figma shortcuts — must use CDP Input.dispatchKeyEvent or toolbar button clicks
File browser uses custom SPA click handlers — need full JS dblclick event sequence to open files
Share dialog toggle: click Share button to open AND close — Escape and canvas click don't work
Main menu auto-closes rapidly — must read items immediately or use direct action buttons instead
Layer rows use data-testid='{nodeId}-layers-panel-row' format for targeting specific layers
Tool activation confirmed via aria-pressed='true', tool auto-deactivates to Move after shape creation
Properties panel inputs respond to browser_fill_form + Enter keydown for value commits
Figma has 3 editor modes: Draw, Design, Dev Mode — switchable via segmented control in toolbelt
